draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces may gravitate toward warm, intimate lighting over cool clinical brightness
facade.label: Warm desk lamp
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should feel personally inviting and focused, not sterile or corporate
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should feel modern, objective, and energetically neutral — comfortable with cooler tones and minimal emotional warmth

facade.hypothesis: Structured layouts with organized data blocks outperform narrative-heavy interfaces for AI workspace users
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data presentation vs narrative flow
facade.label: Dashboard grid
facade.acceptImplies: User values scannability, modularity, and at-a-glance metrics over linear storytelling
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers conversational flow, contextual prose, or story-based information hierarchy over compartmentalized metrics

evidence.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces may gravitate toward warm, intimate lighting over cool clinical brightness
evidence.implication: The workspace should feel personally inviting and focused, not sterile or corporate

facade.hypothesis: The workspace speaks in a friendly, humorous tone rather than corporate formality
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — Playful vs Serious
facade.label: Casual and witty
facade.acceptImplies: Users want their AI tools to feel approachable and human-like, with personality that breaks up heavy work moments
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a neutral, professional tone that stays out of the way and lets the work take center stage

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gradual transitions over instant state changes in an AI workspace, finding them less jarring and more predictable
facade.axisTargeted: Fluid vs snappy — whether interface elements materialize gradually or pop instantly into view
facade.label: Smooth fade in
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace motion design: loading states, entrance animations, cross-fade transitions, and easing curves become core to the experience
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant feedback: no animation overhead, direct state changes, fast snappy interactions that feel responsive and lightweight

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer persistent navigation on the left with content presented as discrete, movable card units rather than full-width panels or tab-based layouts
facade.label: Sidebar with floating cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore multi-panel layouts, draggable card systems, and persistent sidebars as primary organizational metaphor
facade.rejectImplies: Test full-width flows, tab-based switching, or canvas-first layouts where navigation is secondary or contextual

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer high contrast interfaces that reduce eye strain while using color strategically to highlight interactive elements and key workflows.
facade.label: Dark with bright accents
facade.acceptImplies: Explore moody, dark-background designs with neon or vibrant accent colors for CTAs, alerts, and focus areas. Consider midnight + electric blue, charcoal + lime, or similar high-contrast palettes.
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward either full light mode, soft neutral palettes, or muted color schemes. User may prefer either brightness-forward or subdued, non-stimulating aesthetics for sustained focus work.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/supportive tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers information organized by task/workflow vs organized by category/type
Whether user likes instant feedback and snappy responses vs smooth/gradual transitions and animations

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/supportive tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers information organized by task/workflow vs organized by category/type
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes instant feedback and snappy responses vs smooth/gradual transitions and animations
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer gentle, diffused lighting and visual hierarchy over harsh contrast, suggesting a preference for approachable rather than austere interfaces
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace aesthetic should emphasize softness, gradual transitions, and reduced visual strain—layered depth over flat clarity
facade.rejectImplies: Users may actually prefer crisp, high-contrast interfaces that feel more precise and data-forward, suggesting clinical clarity is the right tone for AI tools

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, prompts, and outputs with a clean, productivity-focused design.
